posts.raw_content:
This is the first time our Lord Jesus declared Himself to be the I AM

"And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that comes to me shall never hunger; and he that believes on me shall never thirst." (John 6:35)

In this first "I AM" of our Lord Jesus, our Savior was declaring His in divine identity. He contrasts himself with physical manna, offering eternal sustenance that satisfies spiritual hunger and provides everlasting life rather than just physical nourishment.

Each of these seven “I am” statements follows a basic pattern. They are written as metaphors in which one of the key elements is to reveal that He is the “I AM” who spoke with Moses.

Christ Jesus is our Provider, and Bread of Life. Our Lord Jesus promises that all who freely receive the forgiveness of sins by His shed blood on the Cross, washing all of our sins away, will have this bread eternally in them. He promises His sheep they will never hunger, and as the Water of Life, they will never thirst.

Christ Jesus is our life. "When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall you also appear with him in glory." (Colossians 3:4) Yes, someday we will all meet together, appearing together with our Lord Jesus in Heaven – in our glorified bodies.
posts.author: Come-mistertallyman
posts.title: This is the first time our Lord Jesus declared Himself to be the I AM
posts.content:
This is the first time our Lord Jesus declared Himself to be the I AM

"And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that comes to me shall never hunger; and he that believes on me shall never thirst." (John 6:35)

In this first "I AM" of our Lord Jesus, our Savior was declaring His in divine identity. He contrasts himself with physical manna, offering eternal sustenance that satisfies spiritual hunger and provides everlasting life rather than just physical nourishment.

Each of these seven “I am” statements follows a basic pattern. They are written as metaphors in which one of the key elements is to reveal that He is the “I AM” who spoke with Moses.

Christ Jesus is our Provider, and Bread of Life. Our Lord Jesus promises that all who freely receive the forgiveness of sins by His shed blood on the Cross, washing all of our sins away, will have this bread eternally in them. He promises His sheep they will never hunger, and as the Water of Life, they will never thirst.

Christ Jesus is our life. "When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall you also appear with him in glory." (Colossians 3:4) Yes, someday we will all meet together, appearing together with our Lord Jesus in Heaven – in our glorified bodies.
